text: As I step into this dimly lit, deserted hallway, I feel a sense of uneasy anticipation—a careful mixing of curiosity and isolation. The sterile walls and the quiet hum of the lights remind me of the boundary between the known and the unknown. Entering the exhibition space, I am greeted by the bold colors and intellectual promise of 'Research + Development'. The text and displays invite me to consider the rapidly changing relationship between humans and intelligent machines. Here, I feel a quiet awe mixed with reflective skepticism, aware of the possibilities and uncertainties that technology brings to our world and my own existence.
